**US Finance Executive Senior Executive

Job Summary We are seeking a detail-oriented Finance & Accounts Senior Executive to manage invoicing, accounts receivable and payable, payroll coordination, financial reporting, and compliance activities for our US and Canada operations. The role involves close coordination with business units, consultants, vendors, and external partners to ensure smooth financial operations and timely reporting.

Key Responsibilities

Revenue & Invoicing - Prepare monthly invoice projections and verify new contracts or contract expires. - Coordinate with BU Heads to finalize invoice amounts. - Prepare and issue invoices through accounting systems. - Manage timesheet-based invoicing and project-specific billing. - Track change requests (CR) and coordinate invoicing accordingly.

Accounts Receivable & Collections - Monitor and follow up on customer receivables. - Coordinate with internal teams for overdue invoices and escalations. - Track customer payments and update AR reports.

Accounts Payable & Vendor Management - Coordinate with vendors for invoice collection and processing. - Manage vendor documentation and compliance requirements. - Initiate vendor payments after approvals and share remittance details.

Cash Flow & Financial Monitoring - Prepare weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual cash flow reports. - Monitor receivables and payables to ensure smooth cash flow. - Share financial updates with management.

Payroll Coordination - Prepare payroll data for US and Canada operations on a biweekly basis. - Coordinate with payroll platforms (Justworks, ADP Canada) for payroll processing. - Maintain consultant payroll sheets and support payroll accounting entries.

Financial Reporting & Analysis - Prepare monthly financial reports including P&L and variance analysis. - Maintain dashboards related to services, staffing, and operational metrics. - Support budgeting and revenue projections.

Compliance & Audit Support - Support year-end compliance activities including 1099 and W-2 filings. - Coordinate with auditors for US and Canada audit requirements. - Assist in preparing audit schedules, asset schedules, and year-end adjustments.

Banking & Reconciliation - Perform bank, credit card, and LOC reconciliations. - Track daily bank balances and prepare bank reports.

Operational & Administrative Support - Assist with consultant onboarding and offboarding processes. - Coordinate insurance renewals and employee benefit plans. - Prepare ad-hoc financial and management reports as required.

Required Qualifications - Bachelor’s degree in Commerce / Accounting / Finance - 2–5 years of experience in finance and accounting operations - Strong understanding of AR, AP, payroll, and financial reporting - Experience working with accounting systems such as QuickBooks or similar - Good knowledge of Excel and financial data analysis

Preferred Skills - Exposure to US / Canada accounting processes - Experience coordinating with international payroll platforms - Strong analytical and organizational skills - Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines

Key Competencies - Financial accuracy and attention to detail - Strong communication and coordination skills - Problem-solving and analytical thinking - Ability to work with cross-functional teams

Contact If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at naseeba.parvin@nuvento.com.

Brief Description

We are seeking a skilled L2 Network Support Engineer to join our Network Operations Center (NOC) team. This role is responsible for advanced troubleshooting, incident management, and operational coordination to ensure the stability, performance, and availability of network and infrastructure environments.

As an L2 Engineer, you will act as the primary escalation point for Level 1 support teams, taking ownership of complex incidents and coordinating with L3 engineering teams during major service disruptions. The role requires strong technical problem-solving skills, structured incident handling, and the ability to operate effectively in a 24×7 support environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Incident Management & Resolution
  • Take ownership of incidents escalated from L1 support teams.
  • Investigate and resolve P1/P2 network and infrastructure incidents within defined SLA timelines.
  • Maintain detailed incident timelines, logs, and documentation for operational visibility and reporting.

  • Ensure incidents are managed according to severity and escalation frameworks (P1–P4).

  • Advanced Network Troubleshooting

  • Diagnose and resolve issues related to:
    • Network connectivity and routing
    • Edge infrastructure environments
    • System and platform dependencies
  • Perform root cause analysis using system logs, monitoring tools, and performance metrics.

  • Execute approved remediation steps and recovery procedures.

  • Escalation & Coordination

  • Perform deep technical triage before escalating issues to L3 engineering teams.
  • Clearly define the problem scope, business impact, and affected systems.
  • Provide relevant logs, metrics, and troubleshooting evidence to enable faster resolution.

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to restore services quickly.

  • Major Incident Management

  • Support and coordinate operational response during critical (P1/P2) incidents.
  • Facilitate technical communication between engineering teams.

  • Provide timely updates to stakeholders and operations teams during incident resolution.

  • Continuous Service Improvement

  • Participate in post-incident reviews and root cause analysis.
  • Identify recurring incidents and contribute to preventive improvements and monitoring optimization.
  • Support the development and enhancement of runbooks and operational procedures.

Preferred Skills

  • Strong understanding of network fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, routing, switching).
  • Hands-on experience troubleshooting network connectivity and infrastructure issues.
  • Familiarity with monitoring tools, alerting systems, and infrastructure telemetry.
  • Experience working with incident management and ticketing systems.
  • Ability to analyze system logs, alerts, and performance metrics.
  • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and documentation skills.
  • Excellent communication skills to collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders.

Tools & Technologies

  • Monitoring & Telemetry Platforms
  • Ticketing Systems: Zenduty, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Zoho Desk
  • Collaboration Tools: Microsoft Teams

Preferred Certifications

  • CCNA – Cisco Certified Network Associate
  • CompTIA Network+
  • ITIL Foundation
  • Linux or Cloud certifications are considered an advantage.

Work Environment

  • 24×7 NOC operations environment.

  • Ability to perform effectively in high-pressure operational situations.

Role Overview

The Finance, Compliance & Operations Assistant supports financial management, statutory compliance, procurement coordination, and administrative operations. This role requires strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple responsibilities efficiently. The individual will collaborate with internal teams and external consultants to ensure smooth financial processes, timely compliance, and operational support.

Key Responsibilities

1. Accounts Management & Statutory Compliance

  • Maintain proper books of accounts and financial records.
  • Perform regular bank reconciliations.
  • Manage forex transactions (inward/outward remittances, FX conversions).
  • Prepare monthly customer invoices, track receivables, and follow up on collections.
  • Support financial forecasting, reporting, and cash-flow visibility.
  • Coordinate monthly statutory filings (GST, TDS, PF, ESI, Softex).
  • Track statutory due dates, challans, and maintain audit-ready compliance folders.
  • Collaborate with Chartered Accountants and consultants for compliance.
  • Proficiency in accounting software (Zoho Books preferred).

2. Procurement, Renewals & Payments Support

  • Coordinate procurement (vendor quotations, comparisons, approvals, deliveries).
  • Maintain trackers for payments, renewals, vendors, and invoices.
  • Support payment processing with proper approvals and documentation.
  • Prepare summaries for management on renewals, payments, and procurement status.

3. Administrative & Operational Support

  • Assist HR/Admin with documentation, employee records, and digital archiving.
  • Manage courier handling, dispatch, and official correspondence.
  • Support data entry, spreadsheet updates, and internal system maintenance.

Preferred Skills & Qualifications

  • 1+ years of experience in accounts management, statutory compliance (GST, TDS, PF, ESI), and financial operations.
  • Understanding of accounting principles and financial processes.
  • Knowledge of statutory compliance requirements.
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and MS Office tools.
  • Experience with accounting software (Zoho Books preferred).
  • Strong organizational and documentation skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
  • Excellent communication and coordination skills.
  • Willingness to adapt in a dynamic environment.

Why Work With Us?

  • Meaningful Work: Contribute to platforms used by global leaders like Philips, Hitachi, and Pfizer.
  • People-First Culture: Collaborative, trust-based environment where ideas are valued.
  • Purpose-Driven Technology: Build solutions addressing real operational challenges.
  • Growth & Ownership: Opportunities for continuous learning and career advancement.
  • Global Exposure: Work with diverse teams and clients worldwide.
  • Quality & Trust: ISO-certified processes ensuring high standards and reliability.

Interested candidates should email their detailed resume to careers@ltslean.com with the subject "Application for Finance, Compliance & Operations Assistant".
